top_mistakes.mistake: No discovery questions asked — didn't qualify the lead
Price mentioned too early — establish value first
Customer IS a makeup artist — should upsell advanced courses
Passive ending — put ball in customer's court
Agent speaking 83% of time — listen more
Agent speaking 81% of time — listen more
Agent speaking 94% of time — listen more
Agent speaking 76% of time — listen more
Agent speaking 84% of time — listen more
Agent speaking 69% of time — listen more
Agent speaking 65% of time — listen more
Course dumping — overwhelmed customer with too many options

dos.action: Attempted to book a center visit
Used specific time anchors (today/tomorrow) for closing
Built value by mentioning placement/career outcomes
Gave a specific course recommendation instead of listing all
Maintained good talk/listen ratio (60:40 or better)
Asked qualification questions to understand the prospect

coaching_priorities.note: 60:40 ratio is ideal. Ask more questions.
Keep explanations under 20 seconds. Ask a question.
If stuck: 'Let me check the best option for you...'
Always ask: current level? goal? timeline? budget?
Practice saying 'Lakme Academy' — brand name is critical
Build course value before discussing price
Goal of every call is a visit. Ask: 'Come see the center at 4?'
When customer says they're a pro, pitch advanced/international only
End with firm next step: 'I'll call you tomorrow at 11. OK?'
Don't list all courses. Ask first, recommend ONE.
